title: Lazy Range Iterator
prompt:
Write a Hemlock program that implements a lazy range iterator using closures. Implement:

1. `range(start, end)` - returns an iterator object with a `next()` method that returns the next value or null when exhausted
2. `map_iter(iter, f)` - returns a new iterator that applies f to each element
3. `filter_iter(iter, pred)` - returns a new iterator that skips elements where pred returns false
4. `collect(iter)` - consumes the iterator into an array
5. `to_string_arr(arr)` - joins array elements with ", " and wraps in brackets

Then demonstrate:
- Create range(1, 11), map with fn(x) => x * x, filter where result > 10, collect and print
  Expected: [16, 25, 36, 49, 64, 81, 100]
- Create range(1, 8), filter even numbers, map with fn(x) => x * 3, collect and print
  Expected: [6, 12, 18]
